---
title: "Compact Generative Systems and Residual Honesty"
chapter_id: "compact-generative-systems-and-residual-honesty"
part_id: "routing-compression-evidence-implementation"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "cgs"
  - "rgs"
  - "bugbrain"
  - "simulation_scaling"
  - "rmi"
  - "project_theseus_whitepaper"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# Compact Generative Systems and Residual Honesty

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `compact-generative-systems-and-residual-honesty` |
| Part | Part III - Routing, Compression, Evidence, and Implementation |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `cgs`, `rgs`, `bugbrain`, `simulation_scaling`, `rmi`, `project_theseus_whitepaper` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

The architecture needs a theory of compact systems that generate useful behavior without hiding unresolved complexity.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

Compression can look efficient while moving burden into unmeasured reconstruction, verification, or human review work.

## Core Claim

[compact-generative-systems-and-residual-honesty.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] A compact generative system is useful only when compactness, generativity, governance, verification, and residual burden are all explicit.

## Mechanism

- Define compact generative cores and their residuals.
- Use the seed/router/search/generator/verifier/residual loop as the default compression-control model.
- Treat the raw LLM as a compressed generative engine, not as the entire intelligent system.
- Separate lawful generation from exact remainder.
- Record where compactness fails.

## Interfaces

- Compression feeds memory and routing.
- Evidence tests downstream utility.
- Procedural memory turns repeated generation into tools.

## Invariants

- Lossy claims are labeled.
- Residual burden is visible.
- Fallback exists when compact generation fails.

## Failure modes

- False lossless claims.
- Hidden residual complexity.
- Compactness that damages utility.

## Minimal implementation

A compactness ledger with seed, router/index, search/planning, generator/decoder, verifier/critic, residual correction, memory/governance hooks, exact remainder, verification, and residual fields.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| S/R/Q/G/V/E loop consistency test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Residual burden test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Downstream utility test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Fallback behavior test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `cgs` | Compact Generative Systems | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Smallest adequate structure that can generate/govern target without hiding residual complexity. |
| `rgs` | Ratcheting Generative Systems | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Bridge between active compression, procedural memory, benchmark frontiers, verified AI growth. |
| `bugbrain` | BugBrain | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Edge/resource-aware implementation context. |
| `simulation_scaling` | Simulation Scaling Law | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Resource constraints on scope, clockspeed, and fidelity in simulations. |
| `rmi` | Ratcheting Modular Intelligence | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Benchmark pressure, residual escrow, verified modular capability, regression preservation. |
| `project_theseus_whitepaper` | Project Theseus Whitepaper | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Local-first report-driven RMI implementation reference: SymLiquid, SparkStream, Octopus Router, residual escrow, self-evolution gates, Hive runtime, observability. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
